title: What is Bullpen?
text:
# What is Bullpen?

BullpenFi ("Bullpen") is a non-custodial trading terminal. It brings three markets into one interface:

- **Solana spot trading** — buy and sell Solana tokens directly.
- **Perpetual futures (perps)** — leveraged contracts on assets like BTC, powered by Hyperliquid.
- **Prediction markets** — trade the odds of real-world events, powered by Polymarket.

Non-custodial means you hold your own keys. Bullpen never takes custody of your funds, and Bullpen staff will never ask for your seed phrase.

# Who built Bullpen?

Bullpen was co-founded by Ansem (@blknoiz06), a well-known crypto trader. Ansem co-hosts the "Market Bubble" podcast with FaZe Banks, which is where many new users first hear about the terminal.

title: What is $ANSEM (The Black Bull)?
text:
# What is $ANSEM?

$ANSEM, nicknamed "The Black Bull", is a memecoin on the Solana blockchain.

## Creator-fee airdrops

Trading $ANSEM generates creator fees. Those fees are airdropped back to the community through a claim page on Bullpen. Eligibility is based on social engagement — the wallet you connect is matched with your X (Twitter) activity.

## Claiming

1. Open the $ANSEM Claim page on Bullpen.
2. Connect your Solana wallet.
3. If eligible, your claimable amount appears automatically; confirm the claim transaction.

Claiming only ever requires a wallet connection and a transaction approval. Anyone who asks for your seed phrase to "process a claim" is running a scam.

title: Who is Ansem? Who is FaZe Banks?
text:
# Who is Ansem?

Ansem (@blknoiz06) is a crypto trader and one of the best-known KOLs (key opinion leaders) in the Solana ecosystem. He is a co-founder of Bullpen and co-hosts the "Market Bubble" podcast. The $ANSEM memecoin ("The Black Bull") is named after him.

# Who is FaZe Banks?

FaZe Banks is a content creator and a co-founder of FaZe Clan, one of the biggest names in gaming and creator culture. He co-hosts "Market Bubble" with Ansem. The podcast drives much of Bullpen's retail audience — viewers coming from Twitch, Kick, and YouTube.

# What is Market Bubble?

"Market Bubble" is the podcast hosted by Ansem and FaZe Banks covering crypto markets, trading culture, and the Solana ecosystem.

title: Crypto basics for new Bullpen users
text:
# Crypto basics

## What is a wallet?

A crypto wallet is an app that holds the keys to your funds on a blockchain. On Solana, popular wallets include Phantom, Solflare, and Backpack. Your wallet has a public address (safe to share, like an account number) and a seed phrase (never share — it IS your money).

## What is a seed phrase?

A seed phrase (or recovery phrase) is a list of 12–24 words that can restore your entire wallet. Anyone who has it controls your funds. Never type it into a website, DM, or chat — Bullpen staff will never ask for it.

## What is bridging?

Bridging moves assets from one blockchain to another — for example moving USDC from Ethereum to Solana so you can trade on Bullpen. You use a bridge app, pay a small fee, and receive the asset on the destination chain.

## What is gas?

Gas is the network fee paid to process a transaction. Solana fees are typically fractions of a cent.

## What is slippage?

Slippage is the difference between the price you expect and the price you get when a trade executes, common in fast-moving or low-liquidity markets.

## What is a memecoin?

A memecoin is a token whose value is driven mainly by community, culture, and attention rather than underlying cash flows. They are extremely volatile and can go to zero.

## What is liquidation?

When trading perps with leverage, liquidation happens if the price moves against your position far enough that your margin can no longer cover losses — the position is closed and the margin is lost.

title: Setting up a Solana wallet
text:
# Setting up a Solana wallet

To use Bullpen you need a self-custody Solana wallet. Popular choices are **Phantom**, **Solflare**, and **Backpack**. They are free and work as a browser extension and a mobile app.

## Create a wallet (Phantom example)

1. Install Phantom from the official site (phantom.app) or your device's app store. Always check the URL — fake wallet extensions are a common scam.
2. Open it and choose **Create a new wallet**.
3. It will show you a **secret recovery phrase** (12 words). Write it down on paper and store it offline. This phrase is the only way to recover your wallet — and anyone who has it can take your funds.
4. Confirm the phrase and set a password (the password unlocks the app on this device; it does not replace the recovery phrase).

## Golden rules

- **Never** type your recovery phrase into any website, form, chat, or DM.
- Bullpen — and any legitimate app — will **never** ask for your recovery phrase. A wallet connects by signing, not by revealing its phrase.
- Your **public address** (starts with letters/numbers, e.g. `7xKX...`) is safe to share so people can send you funds.

Once your wallet holds some SOL for network fees, you're ready to connect it to Bullpen.

title: Funding your Solana wallet
text:
# Funding your Solana wallet

To trade on Bullpen you need funds in your Solana wallet — typically **SOL** (also used to pay tiny network fees) and/or **USDC** on Solana.

## Option A — buy on an exchange, then withdraw

1. Buy SOL or USDC on a centralized exchange (Coinbase, Binance, Kraken, etc.).
2. In the exchange's withdraw screen, choose the asset and select the **Solana network**.
3. Paste your wallet's Solana address as the destination and confirm.
4. Funds usually arrive in under a minute. Always send a small test amount first if you're unsure.

> Important: pick the **Solana** network when withdrawing. Sending to a Solana address over the wrong network can lose the funds.

## Option B — on-ramp inside your wallet

Most wallets (Phantom, etc.) have a **Buy** button that lets you purchase SOL with a card or bank transfer via a third-party provider. Fees and limits are set by that provider.

## Always keep a little SOL

Even if you trade in USDC, keep a small amount of **SOL** in the wallet — every Solana transaction needs a fraction of a cent of SOL for network fees. "Insufficient SOL for fees" errors mean you need to top up SOL.

title: Bridging assets to Solana
text:
# Bridging assets to Solana

If your funds are on another chain (e.g. USDC on Ethereum, Base, or Arbitrum), you can **bridge** them to Solana so you can use them on Bullpen.

## How bridging works

1. Open a reputable bridge app.
2. Choose the **source chain** (where your funds are now) and **Solana** as the destination.
3. Connect the wallet on the source chain, enter the amount, and approve the transaction.
4. Wait for the bridge to deliver the asset to your Solana wallet — this can take from seconds to a few minutes depending on the route.

## Tips

- Bridge a **small test amount** first if it's your first time.
- You'll need a little of the source chain's gas token (e.g. ETH on Ethereum) to pay the bridge transaction, and a little **SOL** on the Solana side for fees afterward.
- Only use well-known bridges. Links posted in DMs or random tweets promising "free bridging" or "claim bonus" are a common scam.

Once the funds land in your Solana wallet, connect to Bullpen and trade.

title: Connecting your wallet to Bullpen
text:
# Connecting your wallet to Bullpen

1. Go to the official Bullpen site and open the terminal.
2. Click **Connect Wallet**.
3. Choose your wallet (Phantom, Solflare, Backpack, …).
4. Your wallet pops up asking you to **connect** — this only shares your public address; it does not move funds or reveal your recovery phrase. Approve it.
5. You may be asked to **sign a message** to verify you own the wallet. Signing a message is free, does not spend funds, and is normal. Read what you're signing.

## Connection won't work?

- Make sure your wallet extension is unlocked.
- Refresh the page and try again; if a previous connection is stuck, disconnect the site inside your wallet's **Connected apps** settings, then reconnect.
- On mobile, use the wallet app's built-in browser, or the wallet's WalletConnect / deep-link flow.
- Only ever connect on the official Bullpen domain. If you followed a link from a DM or ad, stop and navigate to the site yourself.

title: Spot trading on Bullpen
text:
# Spot trading on Bullpen

Spot trading means buying or selling a token outright at the current price — you own the token afterward (unlike perps, which are contracts).

## How to place a spot trade

1. Connect your wallet and make sure it holds the asset you want to spend (e.g. SOL or USDC) plus a little SOL for fees.
2. Find the token you want to trade.
3. Enter the amount to buy or sell.
4. Review the quote, including estimated **slippage** — the difference between the shown price and the executed price. In fast or low-liquidity markets slippage is larger.
5. Confirm, then approve the transaction in your wallet.

## Notes

- Memecoins can be extremely volatile and some have very low liquidity, which means high slippage. This is informational, not a recommendation to trade any specific token.
- If a swap fails, it's often due to slippage moving beyond your tolerance or not enough SOL for fees — see the common-errors guide.

title: Perpetual futures (perps) on Bullpen
text:
# Perpetual futures (perps)

Bullpen offers perpetual futures on assets like BTC, powered by Hyperliquid. A perp is a leveraged contract that tracks an asset's price — you don't own the underlying asset.

## Key concepts

- **Leverage** multiplies both gains and losses. 10x leverage means a 1% move in the asset is roughly a 10% move on your margin.
- **Margin** is the collateral backing your position.
- **Liquidation** happens when the price moves against you far enough that your margin can no longer cover the losses — the position is force-closed and you lose the margin. Higher leverage means liquidation is closer to your entry.
- **Funding** is a periodic payment exchanged between long and short traders to keep the perp price near the underlying.

## Risk

Leverage carries real liquidation risk — positions can be wiped out quickly in volatile markets. This is a factual risk disclosure, not advice for or against trading perps or any level of leverage.

title: Prediction markets on Bullpen
text:
# Prediction markets

Bullpen offers prediction markets powered by Polymarket. A prediction market lets you trade on the outcome of a real-world event (for example, an election or a sports result).

## How they work

- Each outcome trades as a share priced between 0 and 1 (often shown as a percentage). The price reflects the market's implied probability of that outcome.
- If the outcome you hold resolves **true**, each share settles at 1; if it resolves **false**, it settles at 0.
- You can usually sell your position before resolution at the current market price.

## Risk

A prediction-market position can go to **zero** if the outcome resolves against you. Prices reflect a crowd's probability estimate, not a guarantee. This is informational only, not advice on any market.

title: How to claim the $ANSEM airdrop
text:
# Claiming the $ANSEM airdrop

$ANSEM creator fees are airdropped back to the community through the official claim page on Bullpen. Eligibility is based on social (X/Twitter) engagement, matched to the wallet you connect.

## Steps

1. Go to the official $ANSEM claim page on Bullpen. Navigate there yourself — do not use links from DMs, replies, or ads.
2. Click **Connect Wallet** and approve the connection (this shares only your public address).
3. If your wallet is eligible, your claimable amount appears automatically.
4. Confirm the **claim transaction** in your wallet. You'll need a little SOL for the network fee.

## What claiming never requires

Claiming only ever needs a wallet connection and a transaction approval. It will **never** ask for your seed phrase, a payment to "unlock" the claim, or a transfer to another wallet. Anything that does is a scam — see the security guide.

title: $ANSEM claim troubleshooting — why does my claim show 0?
text:
# $ANSEM claim troubleshooting

## My claim shows 0 / "not eligible"

Eligibility is based on X (Twitter) engagement matched to the wallet you connect. A zero balance usually means one of:

- The connected wallet isn't the one linked to your eligible activity — try the wallet you intended to use.
- Your account didn't meet the engagement criteria for this round.
- Eligibility is checked per round; a new round may have different rules or timing.

For the exact, current eligibility rules and dates, check the official Bullpen claim page and announcements — those change over time and are the only authoritative source.

## I connected the wrong wallet

Disconnect the site in your wallet's **Connected apps** settings, switch to the correct wallet/account, and reconnect.

## The amount looks wrong or won't confirm

- Make sure you have a little **SOL** for the network fee.
- Refresh and retry; if a transaction is stuck, wait for it to clear before resubmitting.

## Important

Support can help you navigate the flow, but no one legitimate will ask for your seed phrase or ask you to send funds to "verify" or "unlock" a claim.

title: Common errors and how to fix them
text:
# Common errors

## "Insufficient SOL for fees"

Every Solana transaction needs a fraction of a cent of **SOL**. Even if you trade in USDC, keep a small amount of SOL in the wallet. Top up SOL and retry.

## A swap or transaction failed

Common causes:
- **Slippage** moved beyond your tolerance before the trade executed (common with volatile or low-liquidity tokens). Try again, or adjust slippage tolerance if the option is available.
- Not enough SOL for the fee.
- Network congestion — wait a moment and retry.

A failed transaction generally does not spend your traded funds, though a tiny network fee may still apply.

## Wallet won't connect

- Unlock your wallet extension/app first.
- Refresh the page; disconnect the site under your wallet's **Connected apps** and reconnect.
- On mobile, use the wallet app's in-app browser.

## A transaction is "stuck" / pending

Wait for it to confirm or drop before resubmitting, so you don't accidentally send it twice. Solana transactions usually resolve in seconds.

## Still stuck?

Contact official Bullpen support. Never share your seed phrase, and never approve a transaction you don't understand.

title: Staying safe — scams to watch for
text:
# Staying safe from scams

Crypto attracts scammers. Bullpen is non-custodial, so protecting your wallet is on you. These rules keep you safe.

## The one rule that matters most

**Never share your seed phrase / recovery phrase with anyone, for any reason.** Not with "support", not to "verify" a claim, not to "sync" a wallet. Bullpen and every legitimate app will NEVER ask for it. Anyone who does is stealing your funds.

## Common scams

- **Fake claim / airdrop sites** — a site or link (often in DMs, replies, or ads) that looks like the $ANSEM claim page but drains your wallet when you connect and approve. Always navigate to the official Bullpen domain yourself; never trust links from others.
- **Seed-phrase phishing** — a page or person asking you to "import", "validate", or "restore" your wallet by typing your 12–24 words. Never do this.
- **Fake support DMs** — someone messages you first claiming to be Bullpen/Ansem support. Real support does not DM you first asking for wallet access or seed phrases.
- **"Send X to receive Y"** — any claim that you must send funds first to receive an airdrop or unlock a bigger amount. Always a scam.
- **Malicious transaction approvals** — read what your wallet asks you to sign. A drainer tricks you into approving a transfer of your tokens. If a "claim" wants to move your assets out, reject it.

## If you think you're compromised

If you shared your seed phrase or approved a malicious transaction, assume the wallet is compromised: move any remaining funds to a fresh wallet (created on a clean device) immediately, and stop using the old one. A seed phrase, once exposed, can never be made safe again.

title: Fees, limits, and where to find current numbers
text:
# Fees, limits, and official sources

Exact figures — trading fees, deposit minimums, supported bridges and networks, airdrop eligibility thresholds and dates — can change over time. For any specific current number, the **official Bullpen documentation and app are the only authoritative source**; a support assistant should not guess at them.

## General shape (not specific figures)

- **Solana network fees** are paid in SOL and are typically fractions of a cent per transaction. These go to the network, not to Bullpen.
- **Trading** may involve a spread/slippage and any platform or venue fees; check the quote shown before you confirm a trade.
- **Perps** on Hyperliquid and **prediction markets** on Polymarket follow those venues' fee and funding mechanics.

If you need an exact fee, minimum, or eligibility rule, check the official Bullpen docs or ask official support rather than relying on a number from an unofficial source.
